question: What is a “90-degree” release?
content:
A 90‑degree release means the ball is coming off your hand with maximum axis rotation, the ball is spinning almost completely sideways relative to your target line.
Think of it like this:
If 0° = pure forward roll
And 45° = a balanced blend of forward + side roll
Then 90° = almost pure side roll
At 90°, your fingers rotate around the side of the ball so much that the ball’s axis is nearly horizontal to the lane.
A 90 degree release creates:
Maximum skid in the front
Very late hook
Very sharp backend angle
High entry angle potential
High risk of over/under if the lane doesn’t have friction downlane
It’s the most angular release you can make.
